author: @JustavvaGo
text: For me this would mean that we could throw out normal brakes on a car, material wastage disks and pads and, use a ''Coil/Magnet' braking system that doesn't have a lot of wear and tear?   Also at the same time producing electricity/power when the Coil/Magnet brakes aren't being used.   For me ''Food for thought'' Thanks a million.

author: @justtinkering6713
text: That's why shorting the coils acts as a brake.

author: @Ken00001010
text: Just to be clear, it is not the number of coils added in themselves that keep you from turning it, it is the combination of the coils and the loads on those coils. If you put one of your beloved serpentine coils around that wheel, and did not connect it to anything, you could turn it easily, no matter how much wire it had. But, as you let current flow in the coil the torque you have to fight will increase in proportion to that current.
